---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: ../course-outline.css
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    margin: 0.05
    footer: "<a href='javascript:history.back()'>← Back</a>"
---

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>II. Jesus as God</h2>

<details>
<summary><strong>A. Seed of the Woman</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament Prophecy</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A15&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 3:15</a> — "he shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Fulfillments</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+4%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Galatians 4:4</a> — "God sent forth his Son, born of woman…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+16%3A20&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 16:20</a> — "The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+2%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 2:14</a> — "…through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+12%3A9,+17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 12:9, 17</a> — the ancient serpent; war on her offspring</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>B. Seed of Abraham</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament Prophecy</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+22%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 22:18</a> — "in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. See also <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+12%3A3&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 12:3</a>; 17:19</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Fulfillments</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A16&version=WEB" target="_blank">Galatians 3:16</a> — Paul identifies Jesus as the singular seed</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A29&version=WEB" target="_blank">Galatians 3:29</a> — believers share the promise through union with Christ</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+1%3A1&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 1:1</a> — "the son of David, the son of Abraham"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+3%3A34&version=WEB" target="_blank">Luke 3:34</a> — physical descent from Abraham affirmed</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>C. Old Testament Prophecies of a Divine Messiah</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+9%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 9:6</a> — "Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+7%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 7:14</a> — "the virgin shall conceive… Immanuel" (God with us)</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Micah+5%3A2&version=WEB" target="_blank">Micah 5:2</a> — ruler from Bethlehem "whose origin is from of old, from ancient days"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+23%3A5-6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Jeremiah 23:5–6</a> — "The Lord [YHWH] is our righteousness"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>D. Melchizedek — Type of Christ's Eternal Priesthood</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+14%3A18-20&version=WEB" target="_blank">Genesis 14:18–20</a> — king-priest receives tithes from Abraham</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+110%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Psalm 110:4</a> — "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Application (Hebrews)</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A1-3&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 7:1–3</a> — "having neither beginning of days nor end of life…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A15-17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 7:15–17</a> — Jesus arises in the likeness of Melchizedek</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+7%3A24-25&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 7:24–25</a> — "he holds his priesthood permanently… able to save to the uttermost"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>E. The "Son of Man" — Divine Ruler (Daniel 7)</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Daniel+7%3A13-14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Daniel 7:13–14</a> — given everlasting dominion over all peoples and nations</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament — Jesus Claims This Title</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+26%3A64&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 26:64</a> — "you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>F. Jesus' Seven "I AM" Statements in John</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A35&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 6:35</a> — "I am the bread of life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A12&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 8:12</a> — "I am the light of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A9&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 10:9</a> — "I am the door"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A11&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 10:11</a> — "I am the good shepherd"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A25&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 11:25</a> — "I am the resurrection and the life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+14%3A6&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 14:6</a> — "I am the way, and the truth, and the life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+15%3A1&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 15:1</a> — "I am the true vine"</div>

<details>
<summary><strong>Further Study: The Greek <em>egō eimi</em> and Old Testament Background</strong></summary>

<div class="lv-1">1. The Greek Phrase</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. ἐγώ εἰμι (<em>egō eimi</em>) — an emphatic "I am"; Greek normally omits the pronoun since the verb implies it, making its inclusion deliberate</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. ἐγώ (<em>egō</em>) — Strong's G1473 — "I" or "me"; a primary personal pronoun, used emphatically when expressed</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. εἰμι (<em>eimi</em>) — Strong's G1510 — "I am," "to be," "to exist"; first-person singular present indicative of the verb "to be"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. The phrase can mean: "I am" (identity/existence), "It is I" (reassurance), or in absolute uses — a claim echoing divine self-revelation</div>

<div class="lv-1-gap">2. Connection to the Old Testament</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+3%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 3:14</a> — God reveals his name as אֶהְיֶה אֲשֶׁר אֶהְיֶה (<em>ehyeh asher ehyeh</em>, "I AM WHO I AM"), linked to YHWH — eternal, self-existent being</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. YHWH derives from the verb <em>hayah</em> ("to be") — essentially "He is" or "He causes to be" — a deliberate echo of the same root</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Septuagint (LXX) renders <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+3%3A14&version=WEB" target="_blank">Exodus 3:14</a> as ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ὤν (<em>egō eimi ho ōn</em>) — "I am the One Who Is/Existent One" — directly matching John's usage</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. Isaiah's exclusive divine claims use אֲנִי הוּא (<em>ani hu</em>, "I am He"), rendered in LXX as ἐγώ εἰμι:</div>
<div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+43%3A10-11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 43:10–11</a> — "I and only I am God… no savior besides me"</div>
<div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+43%3A25&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 43:25</a> — "I am He who blots out your transgressions"</div>
<div class="lv-detail">— <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+45%3A18&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 45:18</a> — "I am the LORD, and there is no other"</div>

<div class="lv-1-gap">3. John's Absolute Uses</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A58&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 8:58</a> — "Before Abraham was, I am" — no predicate; pure claim to eternal existence</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. The audience's reaction (picking up stones, <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A59&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 8:59</a>) confirms they understood it as a claim to be God</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. The "I and only I" force comes from OT context, not the words alone — Jesus embodies YHWH's self-revealed name</div>

<div class="lv-1-gap">4. Echoes in Revelation</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A4&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 1:4</a> — "from him who is and who was and who is to come"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 1:8</a> — "says the Lord God, who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+4%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Revelation 4:8</a> — "Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come"</div>

</details>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>G. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus' Deity or Worshiping Him</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+20%3A28&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 20:28</a> — Thomas: "My Lord and my God!" (clearest verbal affirmation)</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+16%3A16&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 16:16</a> — Peter: "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+14%3A33&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 14:33</a> — Disciples worship after walking on water: "Truly you are the Son of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A41&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 1:41</a> — Andrew: "We have found the Messiah"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+2%3A11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 2:11</a> — The Magi fall down and worship the infant Jesus</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+28%3A9&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 28:9</a> — Women at the resurrection take hold of his feet and worship him</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+28%3A17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 28:17</a> — The eleven disciples worship at the Great Commission</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+9%3A38&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 9:38</a> — Man born blind: "Lord, I believe." And he worshiped him</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>H. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus as the Christ / Messiah</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A41&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 1:41</a> — Andrew to Peter: "We have found the Messiah"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+8%3A29&version=WEB" target="_blank">Mark 8:29</a> — Peter at Caesarea Philippi: "You are the Christ"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+16%3A16&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 16:16</a> — Peter (fuller): "the Christ, the Son of the living God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A20&version=WEB" target="_blank">Luke 9:20</a> — Peter: "The Christ of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A27&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 11:27</a> — Martha: "you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+4%3A29,+42&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 4:29, 42</a> — Samaritan woman and villagers: "the Savior of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+14%3A33&version=WEB" target="_blank">Matthew 14:33</a> — Disciples: "Truly you are the Son of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+15%3A39&version=WEB" target="_blank">Mark 15:39</a> — Centurion at the cross: "Truly this man was the Son of God!"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>I. Jesus' Pre-Existence Claims</strong> (primarily in John)</summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A58&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 8:58</a> — "before Abraham was, I am"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+17%3A5&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 17:5</a> — "the glory that I had with you before the world existed"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+17%3A24&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 17:24</a> — "you loved me before the foundation of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A38&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 6:38</a> — "I have come down from heaven"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A62&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 6:62</a> — "the Son of Man ascending to where he was before"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+16%3A28&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 16:28</a> — "I came from the Father… going to the Father"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A23&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 8:23</a> — "I am from above… I am not of this world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A13&version=WEB" target="_blank">John 3:13</a> — "he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>J. Additional New Testament Declarations of Jesus as God</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+1%3A17&version=WEB" target="_blank">Colossians 1:17</a> — "he is before all things, and in him all things hold together"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+2%3A9&version=WEB" target="_blank">Colossians 2:9</a> — "the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Philippians+2%3A5-6&version=WEB" target="_blank">Philippians 2:5–6</a> — "in the form of God… equality with God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+1%3A8&version=WEB" target="_blank">Hebrews 1:8</a> — Father speaks of Son: "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+9%3A5&version=WEB" target="_blank">Romans 9:5</a> — "the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+2%3A13&version=WEB" target="_blank">Titus 2:13</a> — "our great God and Savior Jesus Christ"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Philippians+2%3A10-11&version=WEB" target="_blank">Philippians 2:10–11</a> — "every knee should bow… every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord" (<a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+45%3A23&version=WEB" target="_blank">Isaiah 45:23</a> applied to Jesus)</div>
</details>

</section>
```
